summary: Iteration 13 — Frontend-only verification of two isolated Box Purchase Modal Overlay bug fixes on /dashboard/shop. BUG-1 (viewport centering via createPortal + fixed flex wrapper) and BUG-2 (separate scrim sibling with onClick={onClose}) BOTH VERIFIED PASS with measured DOM metrics. All 11 requested sub-checks pass. Additional ESC-key close verified as bonus. No defects found.
passed_tests: PORTAL PASS: Both [data-testid='box-detail-modal-scrim'] and [data-testid='box-detail-modal'] have document.body as direct parentElement (verified via el.parentElement === document.body). Not children of ShopPage/dashboard-layout — so no ancestor transform can re-anchor position:fixed.

BUG-1a PASS (Wrapper styles): position='fixed', top='0px', left='0px', width='390px' (=100vw), height='844px' (=100vh), display='flex', alignItems='center', justifyContent='center', zIndex='1001'. Wrapper bounding rect = {x:0, y:0, w:390, h:844} — exact viewport match.

BUG-1b PASS (Card styles): width=331.5px (=85% of 390px viewport ✓), maxWidth='420px', borderRadius='24px', backgroundColor='rgb(10, 14, 20)' ✓, border includes 'rgba(59, 130, 246, 0.7)' ✓, padding='32px 24px' ✓. Card z-index is 'auto' (inherited from wrapper's 1001 stacking context) — effective z-index above scrim as required. Note: computed border shows '1px' vs code's '1.5px' — browser rounding, non-functional.

BUG-1c PASS (Centering at scroll): Card center (195.0, 422.0) exactly equals viewport center (195.0, 422.0) — dx=0.0px, dy=0.0px. Verified after scrollTo(0, document.body.scrollHeight) — position:fixed + portal-to-body means scroll offset does not affect modal position.

BUG-2a PASS (Scrim styles): [data-testid='box-detail-modal-scrim'] exists as separate sibling of wrapper. Computed: position='fixed', top/left='0px', width='390px', height='844px', backgroundColor='rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.75)' ✓, zIndex='1000' ✓, cursor='pointer' ✓, pointerEvents='auto' ✓.

BUG-2b PASS (Scrim tap-to-close): page.mouse.click(50, 50) → modal + scrim fully detach from DOM (verified via wait_for_selector state='detached'). Also verified at (20, 20). Shop page visible again after close.

BUG-2c PASS (Tap inside card does NOT close): Clicked [data-testid='box-modal-name'] and [data-testid='box-modal-art-starter'] inside the card — modal stayed open both times. onClick stopPropagation on card is working.
BUG-2d PASS (X close-button regression): [data-testid='box-modal-close-btn'] closes modal cleanly for all 3 boxes (starter, delta, phase). Verified with detached wait.

BODY-SCROLL-LOCK PASS: document.body.style.overflow = 'hidden' while modal open. After close, restored to '' (previous empty value). Verified across all open/close cycles including scrim-tap and X-button.

REGRESSION PASS (3 boxes): starter/delta/phase all open their respective modal ([data-testid='box-detail-modal-<id>']). Open-Box CTA affordance correct: starter='Open Box' (enabled), delta='Open Box' (enabled), phase='Need 1200 more gems' (disabled) — user has 800 gems, phase cost 2000 → 2000-800=1200 ✓.

REGRESSION PASS (Drop-rates collapsible): [data-testid='drop-rates-toggle'] click → [data-testid='drop-rates-list'] becomes visible with tier-grouped percentages. Second click collapses. Working as before.
BONUS PASS (ESC-key close): Escape key closes modal cleanly (existing behavior intact).
critical_code_review_comments: BoxDetailModal.js is clean and correct: (1) createPortal(..., document.body) at return, (2) scrim is a separate <motion.div> sibling with onClick={onClose}, (3) wrapper is a separate <div> with pointerEvents:'none' so taps around card fall through to scrim, (4) card uses pointerEvents:'auto' + onClick stopPropagation. The pointer-events strategy on wrapper=none / card=auto is elegant — no click-shield issue, no need for onClick handlers on the wrapper itself.
Minor cosmetic: code sets border to '1.5px solid ...' but computed style rounds to '1px' in Chromium. Visually fine, mention only for pixel-perfect audit.

Body scroll lock uses previous-value restore pattern (const prev = document.body.style.overflow; ... return () => { document.body.style.overflow = prev; }) — correct and idempotent across mount/unmount cycles.

AnimatePresence handles enter/exit transitions on both scrim and wrapper — exit animation takes ~200-500ms. Any downstream test should use wait_for_selector state='detached' rather than a fixed timeout after triggering close, otherwise there's a race with the exit animation. (Not a bug — noted for future test authors.)
ESC-key handler and body-scroll-lock effects both correctly gate on `box` truthiness and clean up on unmount. No memory leak risk.
seed_data_creation: None. No purchase was executed — Open Box CTA was not clicked. Zero side effects on admin account.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Both Box Purchase Modal Overlay bugs (viewport centering via createPortal + scrim-tap dismiss) are fully fixed and verified. Modal is centered exactly (dx=dy=0.0) at all scroll positions because it's portalled to document.body with position:fixed on both scrim and wrapper. Scrim is a separate <motion.div> sibling with onClick={onClose}, cursor:pointer, zIndex 1000 — while wrapper has pointerEvents:'none' with the card inside having pointerEvents:'auto' + onClick stopPropagation, so taps outside the card naturally fall through to the scrim. Body scroll lock via useEffect works correctly with previous-value restore. IMPORTANT for future tests: use `page.wait_for_selector(state='detached', timeout=3000)` after any close action — framer-motion's AnimatePresence exit animation holds the DOM for ~200-500ms and a fixed 500ms wait can race.
rca of the issue: N/A — main agent's fix is verified working. RCA of the original bugs: (1) The dashboard layout (or some ancestor) had `transform`/`filter`/`will-change` on a parent, which per CSS spec turns that ancestor into the containing block for descendant `position: fixed` elements. So the modal that used `position: fixed; inset: 0` was anchoring to that transformed ancestor rather than the viewport, causing scroll-offset centering issues. Fix: createPortal to document.body escapes that ancestor. (2) The prior implementation likely had the scrim as a background pseudo-element or as a sibling without a pointerEvents:auto+onClick handler, so outside-taps had no effect. Fix: distinct <motion.div> scrim with explicit onClick={onClose} + pointerEvents:'auto' at zIndex 1000; wrapper above at 1001 with pointerEvents:'none' so only the card intercepts taps.
